column2: IgA_spline_k = fit_gam_model_kout(data, "IgATiter", "dayofyear") %>% which.min() # the k for the model below.
IgA_spline = gam(log(IgATiter)~s(dayofyear, bs='cc', k=IgA_spline_k), family=gaussian, data = data)
IgG_spline_k = fit_gam_model_kout(data, "IgGTiter", "dayofyear") %>% which.min() # the k for the model below.
# The difference is minimal, so we will take the minimum k
IgG_spline = gam(log(IgGTiter)~s(dayofyear, bs="cc", k=3), family=gaussian, data = data)
IgA_spline_m_k = fit_gam_model_kout(data, "IgATiter", "month") %>% which.min() # the k for the model below.
IgA_spline_m = gam(log(IgATiter)~s(month,bs="cc", k=IgA_spline_m_k), family=gaussian, data = data)
IgG_spline_m_k = fit_gam_model_kout(data, "IgGTiter", "month") %>% which.min() # the k for the model below.
# The difference is minimal, so we will take the minimum k
IgG_spline_m = gam(log(IgGTiter)~s(month,bs="cc", k=IgG_spline_m_k), family=gaussian, data = data)

# Flextable to summarize one model alone (almost an ANOVA table for GAMs)
# Getting iterates out of the models -----
# Summarizing the peaks and the troughs of each model with 95% CIs and Median [IQR]
# IgA_spline_summ_m_min_iqr = IgA_spline_m_pred %>% apply(2, which.min) %>% med_iqr %>% month.abb[.] %>% time_range_concat
IgA_spline_summ_m_min_ci = IgA_spline_m_pred %>% apply(2, which.min) %>% mean_ci %>% month.abb[.] %>% time_range_concat

IgA_spline_summ_m_max_ci = IgA_spline_m_pred %>% apply(2, which.max) %>% mean_ci %>% month.abb[.] %>% time_range_concat

IgG_spline_summ_m_min_ci = IgG_spline_m_pred %>% apply(2, which.min) %>% mean_ci %>% month.abb[.] %>% time_range_concat

IgG_spline_summ_m_max_ci = IgG_spline_m_pred %>% apply(2, which.max) %>% mean_ci %>% month.abb[.] %>% time_range_concat
